explanation: The property requires that in either PCV or PSV ventilation mode, during the expiratory phase, when the operator holds the "Expiratory Pause" button, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves should be closed, and this pause can be held no longer than 60 seconds. We use 'PCVMode | PSVMode' to indicate the system is in either Pressure Controlled Ventilation or Pressure Support Ventilation. The expiratory phase is captured by 'expiratoryPhase'. The activation of the expiratory pause button is 'expiratoryPauseButton', and the closing of both inspiratory and expiratory valves is 'inValveClose & outValveClose'. The 60 second duration is represented by the duration field. The three key decisions are: 1) only in PCV or PSV mode and during expiratory phase and when the button is held, 2) whenever those conditions are met, 3) for at most 60 seconds (within 60 ticks), the valves must remain closed.
decision1_substring: In PCV and PSV mode ... at the end of the expiration phase as long as the operator holds the button
decision2_substring: to press a single button to initiate an Expiratory Pause ... as long as the operator holds the button
decision3_substring: that closes both inspiratory and expiratory valve ... but no longer than 60 sec
output_structured_natural_language: only while (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPhase & expiratoryPauseButton, whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPhase & expiratoryPauseButton, for 60 ticks satisfy inValveClose & outValveClose

explanation: The requirement states that, in PCV or PSV mode, the operator can press and hold a button to initiate an Expiratory Pause action. This Expiratory Pause (expiratoryPause) closes both the inspiratory and expiratory valves (inValveClose & outValveClose) at the end of expiration (expiratoryPhaseEnd), and continues for as long as the button (expiratoryPauseButton) is held, but not longer than 60 seconds. The structured NL translation selects 'while' to express the sustained state while the button is held, and limits the duration to a maximum of 60 ticks (presumably seconds). The boolean expression for the condition restricts applicability to when either PCVMode or PSVMode holds. Additionally, the combined boolean for the controlled state includes the Expiratory Pause being active, the valve states, and the button being pressed. This captures all facets: applicable modes, point of activation, sustained action while the button is pressed, and the valve states held.
decision2_substring: to press a single button to initiate an Expiratory Pause that closes both inspiratory and expiratory valve at the end of the expiration phase
decision3_substring: as long as the operator holds the button but no longer than 60 sec
output_structured_natural_language: while PCVMode | PSVMode, upon expiratoryPauseButton & expiratoryPhaseEnd, for 60 ticks satisfy expiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose & expiratoryPauseButton

explanation: The requirement states that in PCV and PSV ventilator modes, the operator can press and hold a single button (the 'Expiratory Pause' button) at the end of the expiration phase to start an Expiratory Pause, closing both inspiratory and expiratory valves. This action continues as long as the button is held but no longer than 60 seconds. To capture this, I used 'while (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPhaseEnd, whenever expiratoryPauseButton, for N_DURATION ticks satisfy (expiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose)', reflecting (1) the context (PCV/PSV at expiration end), (2) the trigger (button press), and (3) the state (Expiratory Pause: valves closed, for as long as the button is held, up to 60 seconds). PCVMode, PSVMode, expiratoryPhaseEnd, expiratoryPauseButton, expiratoryPause, inValveClose, and outValveClose are all relevant atomic propositions in the dictionary.

decision2_substring: press a single button to initiate an Expiratory Pause
decision3_substring: closes both inspiratory and expiratory valve at the end of the expiration phase as long as the operator holds the button but no longer than 60 sec

explanation: The input requirement specifies that in both "PCV" (Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode) and "PSV" (Pressure Support Ventilation Mode), an operator should be able to press a single button during the 'expiration phase' to cause an Expiratory Pause. This pause involves closing both the inspiratory and expiratory valves for the duration of the button press, up to a maximum of 60 seconds. The atomic propositions relevant from the dictionary are: PCVMode (indicates PCV mode), PSVMode (indicates PSV mode), expiratoryPhase (indicates the expiratory phase is active), expiratoryPauseButton (indicates the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI), inValveClose (indicates inspiratory valve close), outValveClose (indicates expiratory valve close), and user_equalTo_operator (operator is operating the system). The property is captured as: whenever the system is in either PCV or PSV mode and in the expiratory phase, if the operator presses the Expiratory Pause button, both inValveClose and outValveClose must be true throughout the button press, for up to 60 seconds. If the button is released or after 60 seconds, the pause must end. This captures all the specified constraints and conditions outlined in the requirement.

explanation: The structured natural language property captures the requirement that, in PCV or PSV mode, the operator can press a single button to initiate and maintain an Expiratory Pause. This Expiratory Pause causes both the inspiratory and expiratory valves to close (inValveClose and outValveClose), and the pause continues as long as the operator holds the button, up to but not exceeding 60 seconds. The start condition is being in either PCVMode or PSVMode. The trigger for the pause is the expiratoryPauseButton being held. The system must enter a state where both inValveClose and outValveClose are true (both valves closed) and maintain this for as long as the button is held (but no longer than 60 seconds).

explanation: The input requirement states that in both PCV and PSV ventilation modes, by pressing and holding a single button (the 'Expiratory Pause' button), it must be possible to initiate an Expiratory Pause at the end of the expiration phase. While the button is held (but for no more than 60 seconds), both the inspiratory and expiratory valves should remain closed. In the provided atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' and 'PSVMode' represent the respective ventilation modes, 'expiratoryPhaseEnd' marks the end of the expiratory phase, 'expiratoryPauseButton' corresponds to the GUI set Expiratory Pause action, and 'inValveClose' and 'outValveClose' indicate closure of the inspiratory and expiratory valves. Therefore, the structured NL property specifies that whenever (PCVMode OR PSVMode) AND expiratoryPhaseEnd AND the expiratoryPauseButton are true, then for up to 60 ticks (seconds), both valves must be closed, as long as the button is held.

To capture this temporal behavior, we express that when either PCVMode or PSVMode is active and the expiratoryPauseButton is pressed at the end of the expiratory phase, holding the button must keep both inValveClose and outValveClose true (i.e., both valves closed), but this state cannot extend beyond 60 seconds. The selected structured NL expresses: 'while (PCVMode or PSVMode), whenever (expiratoryPauseButton and expiratoryPhaseEnd), for 60 ticks satisfy (inValveClose and outValveClose)', which captures the core intent using available atomic propositions.
